definition: A testable prediction you check with experiments.
example: Her hypothesis predicted faster growth with more light.

definition: A rule violation when a player is ahead of the ball in soccer.
example: The referee waved offside on the scoring play.

definition: Scrambling data so only authorized people can read it.
example: Messengers use encryption to protect chats.

definition: A reaction where a substance loses electrons, often with oxygen.
example: Rust forms when iron undergoes oxidation.

definition: A close relationship between two different species.
example: Clownfish and sea anemones live in symbiosis.

definition: The roof of the mouth; also your sense of taste.
example: The chef trained diners to broaden their palate.
